key_insight: SimPhysNet predicts laser-weld penetration with high accuracy by combining physics-informed self-supervised learning and few-shot classification.
engineering_relevance: The method reduces dependence on large labeled datasets, making automated quality control for laser welding more practical in industrial settings.
method: Use a PINN-guided contrastive self-supervised encoder to learn physically meaningful molten-pool and keyhole features from unlabeled images.
Apply three image augmentation tasks to improve representation robustness and generalization.
Use prototypical-network few-shot learning to classify penetration state from only a small labeled set.
evidence_signal: The model reached 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the unlabeled data, physics priors, and the welding setup used for training.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
